items.title: Well this is my last
items.description: Well this is my last time going here, definitely felt racially profiled while I watched everyone else get seated and got a ticket to pay after they was done eating. As soon as I came through the door they made me pay before I ate. I was the only one they made pay before I sat down to eat. So it’s a GOODBYE TO SUN KING for me. Any recommendation on a good Chinese buffet.

items.title: Appeals court rules Hegseth illegally banned active transgender troops, but can bar new recruits
items.description: A divided federal appeals court panel ruled Monday that the Trump administration is unconstitutionally expelling troops actively serving because they are transgender, but it can enforce its ban against those seeking to enlist.  It’s a mixed result for Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, who has battled in court ever since taking steps last year to effectively bar openly transgender Americans from the nation’s armed forces.  The U.S. Court [...]

items.title: The Spartanburg area gained 400 jobs in March 2026 and has averaged approximately 255 new jobs per month over the past f...
items.description: The Spartanburg area gained 400 jobs in March 2026 and has averaged approximately 255 new jobs per month over the past five years, continuing a broader trend of workforce and economic growth across the region. Job growth impacts more than just businesses. It affects career opportunities, household stability, workforce development and long-term economic momentum across Spartanburg County. Growth conversations are also conversations about jobs, opportunity and the future of our community. Source:

Thank you to our City Ambassadors for helping bring the spirit of Juneteenth to life throughout Spartanburg! #SparatanburgSC #Juneteent

items.description: On Tuesday, City Council unanimously approved first reading of a development agreement for a $71 million mixed-use development to be constructed on the former City Hall block at 145 W Broad St. The development will feature 220 market-rate apartments and nearly 15,000 sq. ft. of commercial space. The project will also wrap a nearly 1,000-space parking garage centered on the block that will serve the development and the new joint City-County local government facility. items.description:
Projects like this are the heartbeat of our firm.

We’re incredibly honored to have been awarded the Georgetown Port Redevelopment Master Plan, helping shape the future of one of the most significant waterfront redevelopment opportunities in the Lowcountry.

For us, this project is about more than redevelopment. It’s about honoring Georgetown’s history, listening to the community, and creating a waterfront destination that feels authentic to the people who call it home. Our goal is to help shape

items.title: Spartanburg City Council approved first reading for the upcoming fiscal year budget at their meeting on Tuesday night. T...
items.description: Spartanburg City Council approved first reading for the upcoming fiscal year budget at their meeting on Tuesday night. The plan holds the property tax rate steady, keeps all fees unchanged, and channels revenue growth into essential public services. Budget projections show roughly five percent revenue growth over the current year's adopted budget, reflecting an expanding commercial tax base that is able to carry a larger share of the City's costs and reducing the tax burden on residential househ

items.title: Strong Towns Spartanburg is hosting a group bike ride called Pedal Burg 🌼! Join us at Fretwell on Wednesday, June 17th ...
items.description:
Strong Towns Spartanburg is hosting a group bike ride called Pedal Burg 🌼! Join us at Fretwell on Wednesday, June 17th at 6:30 pm

We'll start the ride at Fretwell, go through the route twice, and then end back at Fretwell. The ride takes roughly 15-20 minutes and we will be going at a leasurely pace. 
Children are more than welcome to join!

The reasons we are hosting a group bike ride:
1. There is safety in numbers. Having a bunch of people biking at the same time is generally safer around car

items.title: Twenty-five years. Same mission. When JDG opened its doors in Greenville in 2001, we believed architecture could do more...
items.description: Twenty-five years. Same mission. When JDG opened its doors in Greenville in 2001, we believed architecture could do more than shelter — it could restore. Restore well-being. Restore community. Restore our relationship with the places we inhabit. That belief has never wavered. And as we step into our next chapter, we're returning to it with fresh energy and deep gratitude for the Upstate community that has grown alongside us. Here's to the next 25 — together. #JDG #RestorativeDesign #Greenville #

items.title: Three Ways You Can Make Your City a Better Place
items.description: Last month was a super busy month! I have been focusing on The Walking Spartan Instagram while I figure out what else I want to do over here on the Substack. Last month’s back-to-back East Main Road Diet coverage and Amtrak trip to Greenville was so fun but also some of the most energy I have invested into this platform to date. I am immensely grateful for the work that I get to do and all of the great connections I have made through this journey. I figure now is a perfect time to slow down and

items.title: Welcome Kyle to our Charlotte office 🔥 As Survey Crew Chief, Kyle helps lead field operations through detailed data col...
items.description:
Welcome Kyle to our Charlotte office 🔥
 
As Survey Crew Chief, Kyle helps lead field operations through detailed data collection, property research, and equipment management. He brings hands-on surveying experience and a strong technical background, earning degrees from both Clemson University and Coastal Carolina University.
 
When he’s not in the field, you’ll probably find him trail riding dirt bikes or in the gym.
 
Excited to have you on #TeamSW, Kyle!

#SWpoeple #newhire #landsurvey #SWsur

Leadership starts with people. 🎙️

SW+ President Russ Seamon recently joined the People-First Builders Podcast to talk about company culture, mentorship, leadership, and what it means to grow with purpose. Tune in to hear insights on building strong teams and creating a people-first workplace.

Trust us, you’re going to want to hear this one 👀 Listen to the full episode at the link below!

https://open.spotify.com/episode/5QYAHNaoAHsBFCw78gWBS1?si=oFxt77WhQYyUVW2B5W6hfg&nd=1&dlsi=4d33647419eb4b8

items.description: Friday feels like a good excuse to grab a burger downtown 🍔 From pub classics at Delaney’s Irish Pub and Main Street Pub to favorites at Rigsby’s and Cribb’s Kitchen, there’s no shortage of spots to grab a burger and enjoy the outdoors while you’re at it. With good weather, outdoor dining, and local restaurants all within walking distance of each other, we’d say Downtown Spartanburg does weekends pretty well ✨☀️ #downtownspartanburg #experiencespartanburg #theresonlyone

We appreciate everyone sharing their insight and their passions and hope to see everyone at the next special edition meeting 🚲

items.description: Patio season is calling ☀️🍹 If you’re looking for a downtown spot to settle into this spring (and heading into summer), @bluemoonbodega has the vibe covered. From colorful cocktails and refreshing bites to a beautiful outdoor dining space, it’s the kind of place you’ll want to stay all day if you could. Consider this your sign to grab a friend, order something cold, and soak up the warm weather Downtown Spartanburg 🌺🧡 #DowntownSpartanburg #PatioSeason #SpartanburgSC

American Gardens, created in partnership with Beemok Hospitality Collection, transformed a historic Meeting Street site into a vibrant gathering space designed for connection, relaxation, and community with timeless European garden-inspired charm. SeamonWhiteside helped bring the vision to life through civil engineering, landscape architecture, permitting, and site design while navigating the unique requirements of a Special Protection Area in

items.title: We're proud to share one of our most meaningful projects yet — a custom home built on the belief that great design doesn...
items.description: We're proud to share one of our most meaningful projects yet — a custom home built on the belief that great design doesn't dominate the landscape, it honors it. Watch the video to see how restorative design principles and bold architectural vision came together to create something truly special. Every decision — from siting to material selection — was made with one goal in mind: a home that feels like a natural extension of the land it lives on, not an imposition upon it. This is what happens wh

items.title: Introducing Liam 👏 Liam is a graduate of Coastal Carolina University with a degree in Sustainability and Coastal Resili...
items.description:
Introducing Liam 👏
 
Liam is a graduate of Coastal Carolina University with a degree in Sustainability and Coastal Resilience. He is a Surveying Field Technician I on our Charlotte land survey team and focuses on precision, accuracy, and producing work that meets high standards.
 
Outside of work, he enjoys fishing, playing disc golf, hiking, and exploring new places.
 
Glad to have you on the team, Liam!

#SWpeople #landsurveying

items.description: When Dave Edwards stepped into his role as president and CEO at Greenville-Spartanburg International Airport (GSP) in 2009, the airport was much different than it is today. Honoring a legacy that continues to shape our community 🤍

We’re excited to celebrate the groundbreaking of the Kenny Mile Trail, a 1.25-mile multi-use path honoring our Founder, Kenny Seamon’s, legacy and years of dedication to the Town of Mount Pleasant community. Designed to better connect the community, the trail will expand access for pedestrians and cyclists across the area.

This is just the beginning of many community-focused trail projects to come in the Lowcountry, and we’re proud to b

items.title: 🪏 The Town has officially broken ground on the Kenny Mile Trail! 🎉 The Kenny Mile Trail is an approximately 1.25-mile ...
items.description:
🪏 The Town has officially broken ground on the Kenny Mile Trail! 🎉 

The Kenny Mile Trail is an approximately 1.25-mile multi-use path that will connect the existing Park West Trail system to Porcher’s Bluff Road, while also linking to the recently completed All American Boulevard shared-use path. Designed to improve safe, separated access for pedestrians and cyclists, the trail will run largely within the Mount Pleasant Waterworks easement and will be offset more than 60 feet from U.S. Highway

items.description: We're thrilled to join our friends at OneSpartanburg, Inc.  in welcoming Lepore Ristorante Italiano to Downtown Spartanburg! Lepore is open Tuesday through Saturday for lunch from 12 PM-2:30 PM and Tuesday through Saturday for dinner starting at 5 PM. Lepore offers scratch-made classic Italian cuisine and impeccable service in an upscale atmosphere. Be sure to check out their new location at 116 Magnolia Street!

items.description: OneSpartanburg is doing a good bit of "new media" promotion, bringing in social media influencers / bloggers from other cities who then post about the experience to their followers (who will hopefully check us out).  They've also been doing a big sports tourism push for years.  We constantly have youth softball tournaments, and we're getting smaller NCAA events (we had a D1 regional cross country meet a few years ago, and the Big South softball tournament is this week at USC Upstate).  These all

items.title: Downtown Projects & Developments
items.description: I think Spartanburg would be well served by further establishing a 'sense of place' (and, this seems to be deliberately happening right now). Morgan Square is a huge asset. If the city can execute the vision well, this could create the defining image that intrigued people and lives in their minds (in a way that a simple green space + clocktower did not). Personally, I think Spartanburg would be smart to embark on a Greenville-focused marketing campaign. Not in a rival, snarky kind of way (if the

items.title: Downtown Projects & Developments
items.description: I agree with the planetarium and I think a revitalized Barnett Park will be nice.   We need something downtown that is always an attraction like the falls and the bridge in Greenville.  The stadium is an attraction on game day, the planetarium has maybe 2 shows a day, and Barnett Park may have a couple of shows a month if we are lucky.  There needs to be something that is always an attraction. I like Barnett Park, but unless you live here, you could spend all day and never realize it is here.  T

items.title: Who else looks forward to "hanging basket season" each year? 🌼 The weather is gorgeous, the baskets are blooming, and w...
items.description: Who else looks forward to "hanging basket season" each year? 🌼 The weather is gorgeous, the baskets are blooming, and we're so excited to welcome a fun, event-filled spring and summer in Downtown Spartanburg! Thanks to the City's Grounds Maintenance team for hanging and maintaining our baskets.

items.title: Downtown Projects & Developments
items.description: I will say this also led me to read a lot of 2007-2010 posts on this topic and wow, so much has shifted. It was especially interesting to read a version of the likely to happen East Main Road Diet dying back then. I always enjoy diving back in time on this forum. Lots of folks who love cities and love Spartanburg and have literally been on here discussing these things for almost twenty years. Lots of lessons to be learned and inspiration to be found in this forum's backlog.

items.title: Downtown Projects & Developments
items.description: As was stated: Baseball stadium and planetarium. We now also have a proposal to make Barnett Park a better live music venue. Having the Fairfield Inn and Marriott within easy walking distance to the park is a plus. Wish the auditorium 'Hall' would book more bands that are similar to those at the Orange Peel in Asheville.

items.title: Downtown Projects & Developments
items.description: I think Greenville has a huge advantage due to Falls Park in this area, but I think that we are getting there. The new minor league team and the new planetarium are pieces of the puzzle, and I think the next piece will be investing into the "cultural district" (starting with the Barnet Park improvements and going from there). It is a mix between adding new things to our city, as well as refining and investing in what we already have.

items.title: Downtown Projects & Developments
items.description: I think the planetarium has or will boost downtown visitation.

The topic was tourism and things that could be built in downtown Spartanburg to encourage people to come downtown. Other than the ballpark has anything been built downtown in the la

items.description: Our Neighborhood Services Department’s Disaster Assistance Program funds have helped to repair over 80 disaster-related home repairs in the City in the 12 months since the program began. Repairs include tree removal, roof repairs, new roofs, or related work associated with natural disasters (targeting properties significantly impacted by Hurricane Helene in 2024 and other major storms). City residents who have experienced hurricane or natural disaster impacts to their homes and meet income requi

This year’s recipient, Louisa Sterritt, plans to attend the University of South Carolina Upstate. Congrats, Louisa and both our organization and Wade’s wish you the best as a Spartan! Wade's Jr.

items.title: May is Asian American Pacific Islander Heritage Month, a month in which we celebrate the rich history and contributions...
items.description: May is Asian American Pacific Islander Heritage Month, a month in which we celebrate the rich history and contributions of a broad group of individuals from across the Asian continent and Pacific islands. AAPI heritage was initially recognized by Congress in 1979 with the first 10 days in May denoted as "Asian/Pacific American Heritage Week." In 1990, Congress passed a law designating the entire month of May as Asian/Pacific American Heritage Month. May was specifically selected, as it marks the

items.description:
Welcome Isabella Alamilla to SW+! 🎉

A Civil Designer II in our Charlotte office, Isabella brings experience in environmental engineering and land development, with a passion for residential projects and growing the Carolinas.

Outside of work, she enjoys live music, trying new restaurants, and volunteering at her local humane society. We’re excited to have her on the team! 👏

items.title: Downtown Projects & Developments
items.description: I'm having a hard time understanding what all the clocktower fuss is all about.  I suppose the clock itself is historic but the tower surely isn't.  There's nothing about it that shouts history.  Nobody paid much attention to it while it stood in Morgan Square.  Finally, its not "iconic".   Moving it to Barnett Park makes good sense to me.

Our MPS team showed up for #MarathonMonday, an annual tradition inspired by the Boston Marathon that’s all about movement, connection, and a little friendly competition. And this year? The numbers speak for themselves: 👟 278 team members participated 📏 1,048.11 miles walked 🏁 That’s 40 marathons 👣 Nearly 2 MILLION steps From early morning walks to lunchtime loops and after-work miles, this was a firmwide effort that brought peopl

items.title: I Took Amtrak from Spartanburg, SC to Greenville, SC
items.description: The Piedmont & Northern Railway had a passenger rail service from 1914 to 1951. The service connected Spartanburg and Greenville, while also including communities in between such as Taylors, Lyman, Duncan, Greer, and Honea Path. The line also frequently carried freight at the same time as it transported passengers. When the line ended service in 1951, my grandpa was about 13 years old. If he were still alive today I would ask if he remembers ever using this service or if he remembers his father

items.title: At their meeting on Monday, Spartanburg City Council voted 6-1 to advance a plan to rebuild a downtown clock tower at th...
items.description: At their meeting on Monday, Spartanburg City Council voted 6-1 to advance a plan to rebuild a downtown clock tower at the entrance to an updated Barnet Park at E St John and N Converse streets. The new tower will fulfill the City's commitment to rebuild a downtown clock tower to house the historic 1881 bell, along with elements from the previous clock tower that stood in a median along W Main Street. The new structure will continue the long community-centered legacy that has followed the 1881 be

items.title: Last week we celebrated our second annual Decade Club dinner—an evening dedicated to the people who have helped shape SW...
items.description:
Last week we celebrated our second annual Decade Club dinner—an evening dedicated to the people who have helped shape SW+ over the years.

With more than 30 team members marking 10+ years and 6 reaching the incredible milestone of 25+ years, we honored a combined 556 years of service. That legacy of commitment, leadership, and impact is what continues to move us forward.

We were also proud to welcome our newest inductees, Paul Peeples and Tommy Huang, into the Decade Club.

Thank you to everyon

items.description:
Meet Landen 👋
 
Based in our Greenville office, Landen served in the U.S. Navy for six years before earning his degree in cybersecurity. As our IT Coordinator, he helps keep everything running seamlessly behind the scenes, making sure our team has the tools and support they need every day.
 
Outside of work, he enjoys hiking, woodworking, and spending time with his wife and daughter.
 
Welcome to the team!

#SWpeople #newhire #itcoordinator #USNavy
